title: operatingConditions
description: The description of a magnetic operating conditions

name.description: A label that identifies this Operating Conditions

ambientTemperature.description: Temperature of the ambient where the magnetic will operate

ambientRelativeHumidity.description: Relative Humidity of the ambient where the magnetic will operate

cooling.description: Cooling method for the magnetic component
cooling.title: cooling

naturalConvectionCooling.description: Data describing a natural convection cooling
naturalConvectionCooling.title: naturalConvectionCooling

temperature.description: Temperature of the fluid, in Celsius. If absent, ambient temperature is assumed.

forcedConvectionCooling.description: Data describing a forced convection cooling
forcedConvectionCooling.title: forcedConvectionCooling

flowDiameter.description: Diameter of the fluid flow, normally defined as a fan diameter

temperature.description: Temperature of the fluid. To be used only if different from ambient temperature

thermalResistance.description: Bulk thermal resistance of the heat sink. Unit: K/W.

interfaceThermalResistance.description: Thermal resistance of the thermal interface used to connect the device to the heatsink. Unit: K/W.

interfaceThickness.description: Thickness of the thermal interface used to connect the device to the heatsink, in m

dimensions.description: Dimensions of the cube defining the heatsink

coldPlateCooling.description: Data describing a cold plate cooling
coldPlateCooling.title: coldPlateCooling

thermalResistance.description: Bulk thermal resistance of the cold plate. Unit: K/W.

maximumTemperature.description: Maximum temperature of the cold plate. Unit: Celsius.

interfaceThermalResistance.description: Thermal resistance of the thermal interface used to connect the device to the cold plate. Unit: K/W.

interfaceThickness.description: Thickness of the thermal interface used to connect the device to the cold plate, in m

dimensions.description: Dimensions of the cube defining the cold plate
